evidence.claim: Used sentence-transformers, FAISS, Elasticsearch, OpenAI embeddings, Pinecone, XGBoost, LightGBM, Kubeflow, Haystack, YOLO, LoRA, PEFT, QLoRA, PyTorch, pgvector, Weights & Biases
evidence.source_text: Used sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 initially, later upgraded to bge-base) with FAISS... Elasticsearch BM25... OpenAI embeddings... Pinecone... XGBoost and LightGBM... Kubeflow... Haystack... YOLO... LoRA... PEFT... QLoRA... PyTorch... pgvector... Weights & Biases

evidence.claim: Improved search-relevance by 35% over Elasticsearch BM25
evidence.source_text: Reported search-relevance improvement of 35% over the prior Elasticsearch BM25 setup

evidence.claim: Reduced average ticket resolution time by 31%
evidence.source_text: Deployment cut average ticket resolution time by 31% for the supported categories.

evidence.claim: Developed semantic search for internal knowledge base of ~500K documents
evidence.source_text: Developed a semantic search feature for an internal knowledge base of ~500K documents.

evidence.claim: Collaborated with PMs to define optimization targets
evidence.source_text: Worked closely with PMs to define the optimization target (click-through vs. dwell time vs. downstream conversion)

evidence.claim: Owns the evaluation framework for RAG features
evidence.source_text: now own the eval framework for it.

evidence.claim: Earned AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty and Deep Learning Specialization
evidence.source_text: AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty (AWS, 2021); Deep Learning Specialization (Coursera/DeepLearning.AI, 2025)

evidence.claim: Prioritizes evaluation rigor and offline benchmarks
evidence.source_text: I care a lot about evaluation rigor — too many teams ship models without offline benchmarks they trust.

evidence.claim: Values practical experience over theoretical background
evidence.source_text: my main learning has come from shipping real systems and seeing what holds up under production load.
